message:
fix(close,defer,reopen): batch cache rebuild after bulk status changes and modernize test fixtures

- close.rs, defer.rs, reopen.rs: set skip_cache_rebuild=true on
  individual IssueUpdate calls within batch loops, then perform a
  single rebuild_blocked_cache(true) after all updates complete,
  avoiding O(N²) cache rebuilds when closing/deferring/reopening
  multiple issues at once
- sqlite.rs: remove two redundant `continue` statements in retry
  loops (the match arm already falls through to the next iteration)
- sync/history.rs: refactor BackupFileGuard::drop to use let-chain
  syntax (`if let Err(e) = ... && e.kind() != NotFound`) instead
  of nested if/match for cleaner error filtering
- tests/repro_parent_blocking.rs: replace verbose field-by-field
  Issue construction with `..Issue::default()` spread, switch from
  Dependency struct to add_dependency() method signature, use
  ReadyFilters/ReadySortPolicy instead of deprecated ListFilters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,106 +1,45 @@
-use beads_rust::model::{Issue, IssueType, Status, Priority, Dependency, DepType};
-use beads_rust::storage::{SqliteStorage, ListFilters};
+use beads_rust::model::{DependencyType, Issue, IssueType};
+use beads_rust::storage::{ReadyFilters, ReadySortPolicy, SqliteStorage};
 use chrono::Utc;
 
 #[test]
 fn test_parent_blocks_child_ready() {
     let mut storage = SqliteStorage::open_memory().unwrap();
+    let now = Utc::now();
 
     // Create a parent issue
     let parent = Issue {
         id: "bd-parent".to_string(),
         title: "Parent Epic".to_string(),
-        description: None,
         issue_type: IssueType::Epic,
-        status: Status::Open,
-        priority: Priority::Medium,
-        assignee: None,
-        labels: vec![],
-        parent_id: None,
-        created_at: Utc::now(),
-        updated_at: Utc::now(),
-        closed_at: None,
-        defer_until: None,
-        due_at: None,
-        content_hash: None,
-        owner: None,
-        estimated_minutes: None,
+        created_at: now,
+        updated_at: now,
         created_by: Some("tester".to_string()),
-        source_system: None,
-        source_repo: None,
-        external_ref: None,
-        deleted_at: None,
-        deleted_by: None,
-        delete_reason: None,
-        original_type: None,
-        compaction_level: 0,
-        compacted_at: None,
-        compacted_at_commit: None,
-        original_size: 0,
-        sender: None,
-        ephemeral: false,
-        pinned: false,
-        is_template: false,
-        design: None,
-        acceptance_criteria: None,
-        notes: None,
-        closed_by_session: None,
+        ..Issue::default()
     };
     storage.create_issue(&parent, "tester").unwrap();
 
     // Create a child issue
     let child = Issue {
         id: "bd-child".to_string(),
         title: "Child Task".to_string(),
-        description: None,
         issue_type: IssueType::Task,
-        status: Status::Open,
-        priority: Priority::Medium,
-        assignee: None,
-        labels: vec![],
-        parent_id: Some("bd-parent".to_string()),
-        created_at: Utc::now(),
-        updated_at: Utc::now(),
-        closed_at: None,
-        defer_until: None,
-        due_at: None,
-        content_hash: None,
-        owner: None,
-        estimated_minutes: None,
+        created_at: now,
+        updated_at: now,
         created_by: Some("tester".to_string()),
-        source_system: None,
-        source_repo: None,
-        external_ref: None,
-        deleted_at: None,
-        deleted_by: None,
-        delete_reason: None,
-        original_type: None,
-        compaction_level: 0,
-        compacted_at: None,
-        compacted_at_commit: None,
-        original_size: 0,
-        sender: None,
-        ephemeral: false,
-        pinned: false,
-        is_template: false,
-        design: None,
-        acceptance_criteria: None,
-        notes: None,
-        closed_by_session: None,
+        ..Issue::default()
     };
     storage.create_issue(&child, "tester").unwrap();
 
-    // The parent-child relationship is also reflected in the dependencies table
-    let dep = Dependency {
-        issue_id: "bd-child".to_string(),
-        depends_on_id: "bd-parent".to_string(),
-        dep_type: DepType::ParentChild,
-        created_at: Utc::now(),
-        created_by: Some("tester".to_string()),
-        metadata: None,
-        thread_id: None,
-    };
-    storage.add_dependency(&dep, "tester").unwrap();
+    // Parent-child relationships are modeled as dependencies.
+    storage
+        .add_dependency(
+            "bd-child",
+            "bd-parent",
+            DependencyType::ParentChild.as_str(),
+            "tester",
+        )
+        .unwrap();
 
     // Manually trigger cache rebuild
     storage.rebuild_blocked_cache(true).unwrap();
@@ -110,7 +49,9 @@ fn test_parent_blocks_child_ready() {
     assert!(is_blocked, "Child should be blocked by open parent");
 
     // Check if child is in ready issues
-    let ready = storage.get_ready_issues(&ListFilters::default()).unwrap();
+    let ready = storage
+        .get_ready_issues(&ReadyFilters::default(), ReadySortPolicy::default())
+        .unwrap();
     let is_ready = ready.iter().any(|i| i.id == "bd-child");
     assert!(!is_ready, "Child should not be ready when parent is open");
 }
